tokenEstimate.method: Sample TSX files, run `fooks extract --model-payload`, scale sampled bytes to repo-level proxy.

environmentParity.prompt: Identical task text for vanilla and fooks variants.
environmentParity.codexHome: Each variant uses an isolated CODEX_HOME with auth.json and config.toml symlinked from the same host account.
environmentParity.variantDifference: The fooks variant runs fooks init/scan before the same OMX command; vanilla does not.

scale.critical: Do not cite without fixing measurement.

risks.name: sample_size
risks.interpretation: Use as round-1 proof only; repeat N>=3 before claiming stable performance.

risks.name: environment_parity
risks.interpretation: Both variants use OMX exec with isolated CODEX_HOME; fooks additionally prepares .fooks context.

risks.name: cleanup_reproducibility
risks.interpretation: Worktree residue can contaminate reruns if cleanup fails.

risks.name: wall_clock_noise
risks.evidence: Single-run wall-clock timing includes model/service variability.
risks.interpretation: Prefer median and p95 over repeated identical tasks.

risks.name: runtime_token_claim
risks.evidence: Actual OMX/Codex tokens available; fooks used fewer runtime tokens in 1/1 pair (39% less: 57705 -> 34978).
risks.interpretation: Runtime token reduction observed, but N=1 sample. Repeat N>=3 before claiming stable runtime token savings. Keep proxy compression and actual tokens separate in reporting.

tokens.method: Sample TSX files, run `fooks extract --model-payload`, scale sampled bytes to repo-level proxy.

fooks.stdout_tail:
Implemented the account deletion email confirmation improvements.

Modified file:
- `apps/web/modules/account/components/DeleteAccountModal/index.tsx`

Changes made:
- Changed confirmation input to `type="email"`.
- Added inline red error text under the input after the user types an invalid confirmation.
- Distinguishes malformed email from non-matching email using existing translation keys.
- Keeps delete action disabled while:
  - the confirmation email is malformed,
  - the confirmation does not exactly match `user.email`,
  - or deletion is already running.
- Guarded both delete button flow and form submit flow from running while invalid/disabled.

Verification:
- Confirmed only this tracked file is modified via `git diff --name-only`.
- Tried `pnpm exec prettier --check apps/web/modules/account/components/DeleteAccountModal/index.tsx`, but `prettier` was not available in the workspace environment.
